description: Limited-time food promotion posts featuring new menu items or seasonal offerings with specific dates and product highlights for F&B brands targeting Saudi consumers.
structural_recipe: Open with excitement-driven headline announcing new/limited items (e.g., 'Despide el año con...' or 'El menú de...llegó'). Include event emoji (🎇🪩🍽️). Add specific date range in clear format (e.g., 'Dec 29–Jan 3' or 'Nov 3–8'). Structure product details with emoji bullet points listing item names in caps followed by descriptive text. Keep captions moderately long with truncation indicator. Use warm, inviting language emphasizing exclusivity and timeliness.
why_it_works: Saudis respond to time-sensitive promotions tied to celebrations and special occasions. The combination of novelty (new menu items), urgency (limited dates), and visual food presentation creates FOMO while respecting cultural preferences for structured, clear information about offerings.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure date ranges and promotional periods align with local Saudi holidays and shopping patterns
Verify food items comply with halal and cultural dietary preferences
Consider language preference—this pattern may perform better in Spanish/English but test Arabic adaptation
High engagement rate is 0% in observed data; monitor performance before scaling
cultural_constraints: Alcohol and non-halal ingredients should not be featured
Respect prayer times when scheduling posts
Celebration-tied promotions must align with Islamic calendar events
